name: Warrior

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: How would you like the game to be played? Which maps would you like to see the player try on, and how many milliseconds should we wait before showing the next turn?
aliases.type: { maps : List.List Warrior.Map.Map, player : Warrior.PlayerTurnFunction, msPerTurn : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: PlayerTurnFunction
aliases.comment: The type signature of an AI turn function

values.name: program
values.comment: Use this in your main function to start the game.

name: Warrior.Coordinate
comment:
Coordinates are simple records that describes a position on a map

@docs Coordinate

aliases.name: Coordinate
aliases.comment: A Coordinate is the intersection of column x and row y.

name: Warrior.Direction

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment: The different directions a player can move, look and attack. Note that there are no diagonal directions.

values.name: all
values.comment: A simple list containing all Direction values. Handy when combined with `List.filter` or `List.map`

name: Warrior.Item
comment:
A warrior might find and use different items on their quest.

@docs Item

unions.name: Item
unions.comment: The different items that can be encountered and used during the game.

name: Warrior.Map

unions.name: Tile
unions.comment: Describes what is on a specific coordinate of a map.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: Describes how large a map should be in rows and columns. Use this when creating your own maps.

values.name: armLastNpc
values.comment: Places an item into the inventory of the last villain added with the `withNPC` function.

values.name: canMoveOnto
values.comment: Checks if a Move action can be performed.

values.name: canMoveOntoTile
values.comment: Checks if a Move action can be formed onto the given tile.

values.name: coordinateFrom
values.comment: Ignore this function.

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialize an empty map of a given size where every tile is empty. Use the the following `with` functions to make the map more interesting.

values.name: isExitPoint
values.comment: Checks to see if a specific coordinate on the map is an exit point.

values.name: look
values.comment: Provides a list of everything the player can see in a specific direction. The first item of the list will be the one tile away from the player. The second item will be two tiles away, etc.

values.name: npcs
values.comment: Framework function
values.type: Warrior.Map.Map -> List.List ( Warrior.Player.Player, Warrior.Player.Player -> Warrior.Map.Map -> Warrior.Player.Action )

values.name: removeItem
values.comment: Removes an item from the map. This function can be ignored as it will be called by the framework.

values.name: setNpcs
values.comment: Replace all villains with the provided list. Might be less code than using multiple `withNPC` calls.
values.type: List.List ( Warrior.Player.Player, Warrior.Player.Player -> Warrior.Map.Map -> Warrior.Player.Action ) -> Warrior.Map.Map -> Warrior.Map.Map

values.name: spawnPoints
values.comment: A list of all points where players can spawn.

values.name: view
values.comment: Framework function

values.name: withExitPoint
values.comment: Marks a coordinate on the map where the player needs to go to advance to the next map.

values.name: withItem
values.comment: Places an item on the map which can be picked up by players.

values.name: withNPC
values.comment: Places a villain on the specific coordinate of the map, using the supplied function to know what to do each turn. You can find pre-made turn functions in the `Warrior.Npc` module.

values.name: withSpawnPoint
values.comment: Marks a coordinate on the map where the player will spawn.

values.name: withWalledArea
values.comment: Turns every tile between two coordinates into walls.

name: Warrior.Maps

values.name: all
values.comment: A list of all pre-made maps.

values.name: fighting
values.comment: A list of maps which require fighting in addition to navigation.

values.name: movement
values.comment: A list of map that only require navigation.

name: Warrior.Npc.Dummy
comment:
A dummy. A villain which just stands still no matter what happens.

@docs takeTurn

values.name: takeTurn
values.comment: Use this function with the `withNPC` function of the `Map` module to add dangerous opponents to a map.

name: Warrior.Npc.StationaryAttacker
comment:
AI of a villain which attacks anything that gets close.

@docs takeTurn

values.name: takeTurn
values.comment: Use this function with the `withNPC` function of the `Map` module to add dangerous opponents to a map.

name: Warrior.Player

unions.name: Action
unions.comment: The different actions which can be performed on a given turn.

unions.name: Player
unions.comment: This record contains the state of a given player. Playable or non-playable.

unions.name: PlayerRole
unions.comment: This type allows us to seperate playable from non-playable characters. Villains are players which belong to a specific map, where they serve as obstacles.

values.name: addAction
values.comment: Adds an action to the list of previous actions. Is called by the framework and can be safely ignored.

values.name: addItem
values.comment: Add an item to the players inventory. You do not need to concern yourself with this function, as the framework will use this as the result of a Pickup action.

values.name: alive
values.comment: Checks if the player is alive.

values.name: attack
values.comment: This function will be called by the framework as a result of the Attack action, and can be safely ignored.

values.name: attackDamage
values.comment: Returns how much damage this player will do as the result of an attack. This takes the items in the players inventory into account.

values.name: heal
values.comment: This function will be called by the framework as a result of the Heal action, and can be safely ignored.

values.name: health
values.comment: Get the health of a player.

values.name: inventory
values.comment: Get all the items that the player has in their posession.

values.name: maxHealth
values.comment: Get the maximum health the player can have. The player usually starts with this amount of health at the start of a map.

values.name: position
values.comment: Retrieve the current position of a player.

values.name: previousActions
values.comment: Get a list of previous actions, and the player state before those actions occured. The list is in reverse order. Meaning that the previous action performed by the player is the first item. If the list is empty, this must be the first turn of the game.

values.name: spawnHero
values.comment: Initializes a hero. You do not need to concern yourself with this function, as it will be called by the framework.

values.name: spawnVillain
values.comment: Initializes a villain. You do not need to concern yourself with this function, as it will be called by the framework.

values.name: withPosition
values.comment: Change the current position of a player. This will be used by the framework to advance the state of the game. You do not need to concern yourself with this function.
